key_insight: Molten-salt etching of Ti2InB2 with ZnCl2 directly converts the precursor into multilayer Ti2B2Clx MBene with chlorine terminations, removing In and forming no detectable intermediate phase.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical synthesis route and predictive thermodynamic framework for expanding 2D MBene materials with promising battery-relevant performance.
method: Selective molten-salt etching of Ti2InB2 using ZnCl2
STEM/EDX/EELS to confirm In removal and Cl termination coverage
DFT reaction-free-energy analysis to validate formation pathways and competing phases
evidence_signal: In situ XRD showed a direct biphasic transformation, while spectroscopy confirmed complete In removal and Cl coverage of about 1.1–1.4, and initial Li-ion tests showed stable discharge capacity comparable to or better than related borides/MAX phases.
watch_item: The result is demonstrated for a multilayer product with initial electrochemical testing, so scalability, long-term cycling, and extension to other precursors still need verification.
paper_title: Synthesis of Ti2B2Clx MBenes in molten salts from theoretical and experimental perspectives
